headline: What Abbott Laboratories (ABT)'s First Illinois NEC Verdict Means For Shareholders
summary: Earlier in April 2026, a Chicago jury ordered Abbott Laboratories to pay US$70 million in compensatory and punitive damages after finding its Similac Special Care formula for premature infants was defectively designed and carried inadequate warnings about the risk of necrotizing enterocolitis. This verdict, the first Illinois state court decision in a broader wave of more than 1,700 similar lawsuits, raises material legal and reputational questions for Abbott’s nutrition franchise. We’ll now...

headline: Abbott Stock Tumbles on Earnings. Nutrition Business Is Struggling and Merger Costs Biting.
summary: Abbott Laboratories  posted first-quarter earnings that edged past analysts’ estimates but a protracted slump in one business line and a dilutive acquisition sent shares tumbling on Thursday.  Abbott reported adjusted earnings of $1.15 a share, beating Wall Street’s calls for $1.14.  Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.

Sales rose 3.7% on a comparable basis to $11.16 billion, narrowly ahead of the $11 billion analysts had projected.

headline: Abbott Laboratories’s (NYSE:ABT) Q1 CY2026 Sales Top Estimates
summary: Healthcare product and device company Abbott Laboratories (NYSE:ABT) announced better-than-expected revenue in Q1 CY2026, with sales up 7.8% year on year to $11.16 billion. Its non-GAAP profit of $1.15 per share was in line with analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Abbott Stock Falls After Results Show Pressure in Several Businesses
summary: Abbott Laboratories shares are down about 6% after the medical devices and nutrition company gave a 2026 forecast that disappointed analysts.  In its first quarter report released Thursday, analysts flagged challenges in several of Abbott’s businesses including nutrition products and diabetes devices.  The company’s diagnostics unit had lower than expected sales due to weaker demand for tests for flu and other viruses than the year before, and Abbott lost market share for certain heart devices to rival Edwards, JPMorgan analyst Robbie Marcus wrote.

content.title: Abbott Stock Falls After Results Show Pressure in Several Businesses
content.summary: Abbott Laboratories shares are down about 6% after the medical devices and nutrition company gave a 2026 forecast that disappointed analysts.  In its first quarter report released Thursday, analysts flagged challenges in several of Abbott’s businesses including nutrition products and diabetes devices.  The company’s diagnostics unit had lower than expected sales due to weaker demand for tests for flu and other viruses than the year before, and Abbott lost market share for certain heart devices to rival Edwards, JPMorgan analyst Robbie Marcus wrote.
